analysis_steps: Identified two peaks in the 350–420 cm⁻¹ region, characteristic of transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs).
Matched 380 cm⁻¹ peak to MoS2 E₂g¹ mode (literature: ~383 cm⁻¹ for bulk, ~384 cm⁻¹ downshifts to ~380 for monolayer).
Matched 406 cm⁻¹ peak to MoS2 A₁g mode (literature: ~408 cm⁻¹ for bulk, red-shifts to ~405–406 for monolayer).

Computed peak separation Δ = 26 cm⁻¹; monolayer MoS2 typically shows Δ ≈ 18–21 cm⁻¹ in ideal CVD samples but can be ~25–26 in exfoliated; this is below the bulk value of ~25–26 cm⁻¹, pointing to monolayer or thin few-layer.
Ruled out WS2 (E₂g¹ ~356 cm⁻¹, A₁g ~421 cm⁻¹) and MoSe2 (lower frequency modes ~240–290 cm⁻¹) based on peak positions.
Narrow FWHM and absence of additional modes confirm single-phase crystalline MoS2 with no contamination.
Relative intensity ratio (E₂g¹ >> A₁g) is consistent with monolayer geometry where in-plane mode dominates.
Final assignment: monolayer crystalline MoS2 with high confidence.
decision_trace: Peak at 380 cm⁻¹ matches MoS2 E₂g¹ in-plane mode
Peak at 406 cm⁻¹ matches MoS2 A₁g out-of-plane mode
Peak separation Δ ≈ 26 cm⁻¹ is below the bulk value (~25–26 cm⁻¹), strongly suggestive of monolayer
Sharp FWHM (~8 cm⁻¹) indicates high crystallinity
No extra peaks observed that would indicate contamination or secondary phases
E₂g¹ prominence (0.53) > A₁g prominence (0.15), consistent with monolayer intensity ratios
